title: Omnibus Bill — Pre-Enactment Single-Subject Challenge
description: Congress passes a large omnibus bill containing provisions on unrelated subjects — defense appropriations, immigration rules, and drug pricing — bundled into a single bill in violation of Article II Section 10.7. Before the President can act on it, a member invokes the pre-enactment challenge mechanism of Article II Section 10.7A. The Supreme Court rules within the required 15-day window, finds a single-subject violation, and returns the bill to the originating chamber for severance or redrafting. The 15-day presidential action clock is tolled pending the ruling.
